title: eriogonum inerme subsp. hispidulum
intro: Eriogonum inerme subsp. hispidulum, also known as desert buckwheat, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Eriogonum inerme subsp. hispidulum include infusions, teas, and salves that can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
